summary: Full-stack testing of OnboardingCelebration feature (confetti + congrats modal that fires once when all 5 setup items are done). Backend: 7/7 new pytest cases pass in test_onboarding_celebrated.py (status returns 'celebrated' bool, PUT accepts {celebrated:bool}, partial updates don't clobber welcome_seen, fresh-user E2E with mongo-seeded card_settings/clients/quotes reaches completed=true while celebrated=false, simulated frontend PUT persists celebrated=true). Backend regression: 8/8 in test_onboarding.py still pass. Frontend (Playwright): registered a fresh user, seeded MongoDB so all 5 items are done, logged in via UI — onboarding-celebration modal appeared immediately, confetti rendered without console errors, PUT /api/onboarding/state {celebrated:true} fired automatically (verified via request interception), 'Seguir explorando' (celebration-close) closed modal, reload did NOT re-show the modal (persistence works), 'Crear mi primer quote con AI' (celebration-create-quote) navigated to /quotes/nuevo?ai=1. Regression on Super Admin (pzsuave007): WelcomeModal + SetupChecklist still render correctly, celebration modal does NOT fire for incomplete user — all data-testids preserved (welcome-explore, welcome-start, welcome-close, checklist-progress, checklist-dismiss).
action_items: No blocking issues. Feature is production-ready.
critical_code_review_comments: OnboardingCelebration.js fires the PUT {celebrated:true} BEFORE setting state, with the body wrapped in `try{...}catch{}` that swallows errors. Acceptable for UX (don't block confetti on a transient network blip), but if the PUT fails the user will see the modal again on next login. Consider a single quiet retry on failure.

useEffect depends on `location.key` — every SPA navigation back to Dashboard re-runs the status check. The `firedRef` guard prevents re-firing within the same session, but a different mount (e.g. logout/login in same tab) will hit `/onboarding/status` again. Acceptable today; cache via React Query if dashboard load becomes hot.
Backend correctly defaults `celebrated=false` for legacy users via `bool(onb.get('celebrated'))` — no migration needed. Good.

Confetti is launched via raw `requestAnimationFrame` with no cancel on unmount. If the user navigates away within the 2.5s loop, frames keep firing on a (now hidden) canvas. Minor — canvas-confetti cleans up its own canvas, but a `cancelAnimationFrame` ref would be cleaner.
The modal Trophy/PartyPopper graphics and Spanish copy ('¡Tu negocio está listo!', 'Diste el paso más difícil') match the spec — no copy issues.
test_credentials: Super Admin pzsuave007@gmail.com / Uni2mkt007! works. Test user test_celeb_ui_86b8c23a@example.com was created and cleaned up after run.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up. During testing seeded a fresh user (test_celeb_ui_*@example.com) with: business_address set, card_settings {logo_photo_id, profile_photo_id, services:[...]}, 1 client doc, 1 quote doc — to drive done_count=5/completed=true. All cleaned up at end of run (users, card_settings, clients, quotes prefixed with this user_id deleted). Super Admin onboarding_state reset to {welcome_seen:false, dismissed:false, celebrated:false} for the next agent / human run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Onboarding celebration feature shipped clean. To re-verify quickly: (1) backend — run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_onboarding_celebrated.py -v` (auto-creates + cleans a fresh user); (2) UI — pzsuave007 has done_count=1 so celebration will NOT fire for that account. To force the celebration modal again, either (a) seed a new user the same way the test does or (b) for pzsuave007 set business_address, insert card_settings with services+logo_photo_id, insert 1 client + 1 quote, then login. Super Admin onboarding_state was reset to defaults at end of this iteration. No code changes were made — only test files added.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Feature works end-to-end as specified.
